title: Moonlit Chawan
story: Thrown and fired in a single autumn session. The surface bears the trace of pine ash and the crackle of rapid cooling — a bowl meant for winter tea gatherings.

title: Winter Ash Bowl
story: Named for the grey-white ash glaze that settled during firing. Used in Nakamura-san's own tea room before being offered for acquisition.

title: Golden Raku Cup
story: A small yunomi with kintsugi gold repair along one edge — a deliberate embrace of imperfection.

title: Cobalt Scroll Vase
story: Hand-painted with scrolling vine motifs in Yunnan cobalt. Fired at 1,320°C for a depth of blue that shifts with the light.

title: Lotus Plate
story: A display plate painted with lotus blossoms and herons, inspired by Ming dynasty export ware.

title: Blue Scroll Teapot
story: A functional teapot with underglaze blue landscape decoration. The spout pours without dripping — a test Wei Lin applies to every piece.

title: Shigaraki Storage Jar
story: A large tsubo jar fired for five days in the anagama. Natural ash created a gradient from charcoal grey to warm orange.

title: Ash Glaze Bowl
story: A serving bowl with thick natural ash glaze pooling in the interior. Intended for seasonal kaiseki presentations.

title: Flame Vessel
story: Sculptural form inspired by the flame patterns left on kiln shelves. One of a series of twelve.

title: Crane & Pine Panel
story: Double-sided embroidery depicting cranes among ancient pines. Over 12,000 individual stitches on natural silk gauze.

title: Misty Landscape
story: A Song-inspired landscape rendered in graduated silk tones. Commissioned for a private collection in London.

title: Hagi Tea Bowl
story: A classic Hagi chawan with soft white glaze. The clay body will deepen with tea use over years of ceremony.

title: Biwa Yunomi
story: A daily tea cup in the warm loquat-toned glaze unique to Hagi ware. Comfortable in the hand, subtle in the mouth.

title: Jade Gu Vase
story: A gu-form vase with the characteristic Longquan jade-green glaze. Reduction-fired to achieve the "secret colour."

title: Celadon Bowl
story: A simple bowl form that showcases the depth of the celadon glaze. The interior catches light like still water.

title: Gold Brocade Panel
story: A Nishijin-ori panel woven with gold-leaf threads on a deep indigo ground. Traditional peony motif reinterpreted at reduced scale.

title: Crane Obi Art
story: A section of obi brocade featuring flying cranes, framed as wall art. Woven on a hand-operated Jacquard loom.

title: Silk Wall Hanging
story: A contemporary wall hanging combining Nishijin brocade with hand-embroidered accents. Created in collaboration with a Kyoto painter.
